emergencies.title: 41 concurrent national emergencies under the NEA (18 declared by Trump)

source_urls.title: Official source

emergencies.notes: As of 2026-03-16, 41 of 93 total NEA emergencies remain in effect. 18 were declared by Trump. As of mid-2025, 51 national emergencies are simultaneously in effect. Trump declared 9 new emergencies in his second term covering the southern border (Proc. 10886), energy (EO 14156), cartel designations (EO 14157), northern border drugs (EO 14193), tariffs on Mexico/Canada/China, cybersecurity, ICC sanctions (EO 14203), reciprocal tariffs (EO 14257), and Brazil sanctions (EO 14323). Two first-term emergencies also remain active: Global Magnitsky (EO 13818, 2017) and foreign election interference (EO 13848, 2018). Critics argue most second-term declarations are designed to bypass Congress on domestic policy rather than address genuine emergencies.
emergencies.legal_basis: National Emergencies Act of 1976 (50 U.S.C. §1601); IEEPA (50 U.S.C. §1701)

emergencies.title: Continuous state of emergency since founding in 1948 (renewed annually by Knesset)

source_urls.title: Official source

emergencies.notes: Israel's state of emergency has been in continuous effect since founding. Renewed annually by Knesset vote. Empowers government to issue emergency regulations, including administrative detention. The world's longest continuously active state of emergency.
emergencies.legal_basis: Law and Administration Ordinance 1948 §9; Basic Law: The Government (2001) §38-39

emergencies.title: State of emergency since 1962 Brunei revolt (never lifted, Sultan rules by decree)

source_urls.title: Official source

emergencies.notes: State of emergency invoked by Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III on Dec 12, 1962 in response to the Brunei revolt. Never lifted. Sultan rules by decree; elected Legislative Council dissolved. Emergency renewed every 2 years. The world's second-longest continuously active state of emergency.

all_emergencies.notes: On 17 March 2020, Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte declared all of the Philippinesto be in a state of calamity following a dramatic rise in cases of COVID-19, the declaration was eventually lifted in September 2022.

all_emergencies.notes: On 13 March 2020, Prime Minister of Spain Pedro Sánchez announced the declaration of the state of emergency in the nation for a period of 15 days, to become effective next day after the approval of the Council of Ministersfor the COVID-19 pandemic in Spain.
